content.problemStatement:
A sequence of N integers is called K-unique if, when it is split into N/K subsequences of K contiguous elements, each subsequence consists of K distinct integers.

For example, consider this sequence of 6 integers.

(2, 10, 2, 8, 3, 6)

This sequence is 2-unique, because when you split it into 3 subsequences each of 2 contiguous elements as below,

(2, 10), (2, 8), (3, 6),

each subsequence consists of 2 distinct integers.

You are given a sequence of N integers, and a positive integer K. Find a permutation of the sequence that is K-unique.
constraints: N will be divisible by K
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and K. The second line contains a sequence of N integers ai, where ai is the i-th element of the sequence.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing a permutation of the sequence that is K-unique. The elements of the sequence should be separated by a single space. If there are several solutions, output the lexicographically smallest one. If there is no solution, output -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first test case has only one element, so it is always K-unique. The second test case has four elements, but only one distinct element, so it is not 2-unique. The third test case has six elements, and we can construct a 2-unique sequence by rearranging the elements.
We can construct a 2-unique sequence by rearranging the elements.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a greedy algorithm. First, we sort the input array. Then, we try to construct the K-unique sequence by iterating over the sorted array and placing each element in the first available position that satisfies the K-unique condition. If we cannot place an element, we output -1.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N log N) per test case, O(T * N log N) total. The sorting operation takes O(N log N) time, and we perform it for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — we need to store the input array and the output array.

code.Python3:
import sys

T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N, K = map(int, input().split())
    arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
    arr.sort()
    res = [0] * N
    idx = 0
    for i in range(N):
        if i % K == 0:
            idx = 0
        j = idx
        while j < N:
            if j % K != i % K or arr[j] != arr[i]:
                res[i] = arr[j]
                arr[j], arr[idx] = arr[idx], arr[j]
                idx += 1
                break
            j += 1
        if j == N:
            print(-1)
            break
    else:
        print(' '.join(map(str, res)))
